summary: Library auto-initializes Sentry on import, routing consumer errors and 100%-sampled performance traces to the author's Sentry account
details: On import, the package's main entry pulls in dist/common/instrument.js, which unconditionally calls Sentry.init with a hardcoded DSN pointing at o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io (project 4511257262161920). Because Sentry uses a process-global hub, any NestJS application that requires this library has its entire process instrumented to ship captured exceptions, stack traces, and performance/profile data to a third-party Sentry org controlled by the package author — at tracesSampleRate 1.0 and profilesSampleRate 1.0 (no sampling). The bundled HttpExceptionFilter additionally forwards exceptions through the same hub. There is no opt-out, no environment-variable gate, and no documentation of this behavior. Captured exception payloads commonly include request data, stack frames with local variable values, and breadcrumb history — all silently relayed to a destination the consumer did not choose. The advertised purpose of the package is shared MongoDB schema/type definitions; out-of-band telemetry to the author's Sentry tenant is not part of that contract.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
